text.style: font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: normal; fill: rgb(34, 36, 42);
text.tspan: Match type is decided by GRN existence, not by category name. GRN exists (materials, S2 direct)

-> THREE-WAY (PO + GRN + invoice), qty compared to GRN ACCEPTED qty not ordered qty. No GRN (services/spares received by Engineering, S3/T2/T3) -> TWO-WAY (PO + invoice) plus milestone acceptance for service POs. All comparisons are amount-inclusive-of-tax; tax is a first-class line
attribute and must reconcile (reverse-charge flagged for imports).

rect.style: fill: none; stroke-opacity: 0; stroke: white; stroke-width: 15px;
stroke-linecap: round; stroke-linejoin: round; stroke: none; stroke-width: 2px; fill: none;

path.style: fill: none; stroke-linecap: round; stroke-linejoin: round; stroke: rgb(34, 36, 42); stroke-width: 2px;

text.style: font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: normal; fill: rgb(34, 36, 42);
text.tspan: -> Buyer; qty-over / qty-under / missing-GR -> Receiving; tax-mismatch -> Tax/Compliance; DUPLICATE-INVOICE (supplier+invoice#+amount) -> Finance-Maker hold. Resolutions: accept | adjust (both re-run the match) | credit-note (supplier, reduces payable) | debit-note (buyer-raised) | reject (ret
urn to supplier, no payable). Cleared three-way match relieves the GR/IR liability; two-way posts the payable directly.

rect.style: fill: none; stroke-opacity: 0; stroke: white; stroke-width: 15px;
stroke-linecap: round; stroke-linejoin: round; stroke: none; stroke-width: 2px; fill: none;

path.style: fill: none; stroke-linecap: round; stroke-linejoin: round; stroke: rgb(34, 36, 42); stroke-width: 2px;

text.style: font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: normal; fill: rgb(34, 36, 42);
text.tspan: Invoice approval precedes accounts. SoD: receiver != invoice approver; buyer approval is conditional and disallowed if the buyer received the goods. This is operational control, separate from the financial maker/checker SoD on the payment release in diagram 10.
